title: Star Wars: Chewbacca (2000)
content:
{{Top|rwm|njo|italics=1}} {{Youmay|the comic series|the Wookiee [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] or the [[Marvel Comics|Marvel]] [[Star Wars: Chewbacca (2015)|miniseries]]}} {{Otheruses|Chewbacca}} {{ComicSeries |image=[[File:SWChewbacca1-FC.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars: Chewbacca'' |writer=[[Darko Macan]] |penciller=Various |inker= |letterer= |colorist= |cover artist=[[Sean Phillips]] |editor= |publisher=[[Dark Horse Comics]] |start date=[[January 19]], [[2000]]''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, Updated and Expanded Edition]]'' |end date=[[April 19]], [[2000]]{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54037/star_wars_chewbacca_2000_4|text=Star Wars: Chewbacca (2000) #4}} |schedule=Monthly |format=Completed |issues=4 |timeline=[[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' |series= |preceded by= |followed by= }} '''''Star Wars: Chewbacca''''' is a series of [[Star Wars comic books|comics]] featuring reminiscences from the title [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca's]] friends after his death on [[Sernpidal/Legends|Sernpidal]] in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]]. The series is composed of four issues released in January through April [[2000]], with the trade paperback edition being released in January [[2001]]. ==Publisher's summary== ''When the ''Star Wars'' galaxy's greatest Wookiee met his heroic end in the ''New Jedi Order'' novel ''The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime'', shockwaves ran through the ranks of ''Star Wars'' devotees everywhere. And now Dark Horse presents a commemorative collection detailing, for the first time ever, the history of the mighty Chewbacca! Written by longtime Star Wars writer Darko Macan, Chewbacca features a series of tales narrated by the characters who knew him best—including Lando Calrissian, Princess Leia, Han Solo, and Luke Skywalker—that reveals special glimpses not only of Chewbacca's heroic history, but of the mysterious and fascinating culture of his noble species. Don't miss the opportunity to say goodbye and farewell to this beloved character!'' ==Plot summary== Still reeling from Chewbacca's death, R2-D2 and C-3PO decide to scout around and find out more about the mighty wookiee warrior. They get accounts from such characters; as Luke Skywalker, Han Solo, Leia Organa-Solo, Lando Calrissian, Wedge Antilles, Attichitcuk, Mallatobuck, a bounty hunter, and a Trandoshan slaver. Includes an insight into Han Solo's time as an Imperial Lieutenant! ==Continuity== Following Dark Horse's introduction of [[Nom Anor]] in [[Star Wars: Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood|''Crimson Empire II'']], they had planned to do a [[Dark Horse invasion storyline|massive comics arc]] (in similar style to their later Clone Wars arc in ''[[Star Wars: Republic]]'') detailing a Galactic invasion by a species (rumored to be either beings made of liquid or energy) from the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]], of which Nom Anor was an advanced scout. The death of Chewbacca was planned to occur early on, but other than that, nothing was known for sure. Lucasfilm, while impressed by the idea, gradually decided to turn it over to their book division, where it eventually became the [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''New Jedi Order'' series]]. When they saw what little involvement they would have in the series that they had developed, Dark Horse opted out almost altogether, retaining only the Chewbacca death story—which, after being transferred to ''Vector Prime'', became this tribute comic. [[Wikipedia:Crow T. Robot|Crow T. Robot]] and Tom Servo of the cult program [[Wikipedia:Mystery Science Theater 3000|''Mystery Science Theater 3000'']] makes an appearance as one of the scrapped [[Droid/Legends|droids]] when [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina]] is abducted by a crazed droid in [[Chewbacca (2000) 4|''Chewbacca'' (2000) 4]]. ==Media== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Star Wars: Chewbacca (2000)}} ===Issues=== {| {{Prettytable}} ! colspan="3" |Color code key: |- |style="background-color:#FFBB60;" align="center" width="33%" |Collected (TPB) issue |style="background-color:#40CC40;" align="center" width="33%" |Released issue |style="background-color:#FFFF80;" align="center" width="33%" |Future issue |} {| {{Prettytable}} !
